title: The Softmax Weighting
concept: Passing similarity scores through a softmax function converts them into probabilities that sum to 1.0, creating clean attention weights. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Self-Attention.'

exercises.prompt: To make attention weights useful, we need to turn raw similarity scores into what?
exercises.explain: Before we can decide how much attention to pay to different words, we need to convert our messy raw comparison scores into clean percentages that total up to 1.0.

cards.title: Cleaning Up the Scores
cards.body:
When an AI compares words, it gets raw similarity scores like 15.4, -2.1, and 8.0. How does the AI make sense of these wild numbers to decide where to focus its attention? 

It uses a mathematical magic trick called **Softmax** to clean up the mess and turn them into perfect percentages!

cards.title: What is Softmax?
cards.body:
The **Softmax function** is a mathematical recipe that takes a list of raw similarity scores and turns them into probabilities (percentages). 

No matter how large or small the initial scores are, Softmax squash-scales them so they are all between 0 and 1, and most importantly, they all **add up to exactly 1.0 (or 100%)**.

cards.title: The Pizza Slices analogy
cards.body:
Imagine you are distributing 10 slices of pizza among friends based on how hungry they say they are. If you just look at their hunger scores on a scale of 1 to 100, it's hard to divide the pizza fairly. 

The Softmax function acts like a fair host: it takes everyone's hunger scores and translates them into exact percentages of the pizza, ensuring that all the slices distributed add up to exactly one whole pizza!

cards.title: Softmax in Action
cards.body:
Suppose a word gets similarity scores of **[2.0, 1.0, 0.0]** with three other words. 

If we pass these raw numbers through Softmax, they turn into clean attention weights:
• **0.67** (67% attention)
• **0.24** (24% attention)
• **0.09** (9% attention)

All three weights add up to exactly **1.0**, telling the AI exactly how to distribute its focus.

cards.title: Does Softmax pick only one?
cards.body:
**Myth:** Softmax just picks the single highest score and ignores all the other scores.

**Reality:** Softmax keeps all the options in play! While it does give the highest score the biggest slice of the pie, it still assigns smaller, proportional slices to the other scores so no context is completely forgotten.

cards.title: Before vs. After Softmax
cards.body: See how Softmax transforms messy, unpredictable raw scores into neat, standardized attention weights that are easy for the AI to compute.

right.text: Values like [0.98, 0.02, 0.00] that represent clean percentages adding up to 1.0.

left.text: Values like [4.5, 0.5, -2.1] that are hard to compare directly and don't have a fixed limit.

exercises.prompt: What is always true about the attention weights output by the Softmax function?

options.text: They are between 0 and 1, and they sum to 1.0.
They can be any numbers, as long as they sum to 100.
They are all negative numbers that sum to -1.0.
They are always equal to each other.

exercises.explain: Softmax outputs are mathematical probabilities, meaning every value is between 0 and 1, and the total sum of all values is always exactly 1.0 (or 100%).

exercises.prompt: True or False: The output values of a Softmax function can all be greater than 1.0 at the same time.
exercises.explain: Because Softmax outputs must sum to 1.0, they cannot all be greater than 1.0. Typically, they are decimals like 0.7, 0.2, and 0.1.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe what Softmax creates.
exercises.sentence: The Softmax function turns raw similarity scores into probabilities that sum to 1.0.
exercises.explain: Softmax converts raw similarity numbers into clean probabilities that range between 0 and 1 and sum up to 1.0.

exercises.prompt: Which AI assistant correctly explains the purpose of the Softmax function?

options.text: AI A says: "Softmax simply multiplies all similarity scores by 10 to make them easier for the network to read."
AI B says: "Softmax converts raw similarity scores into a set of weights between 0 and 1 that sum up to 1.0."
AI C says: "Softmax converts all numbers into binary zeros and ones, keeping only the maximum value."

exercises.explain: AI B correctly explains that Softmax turns raw scores into weights that sum to 1.0. AI A incorrectly claims it makes scores larger, and AI C incorrectly claims it converts numbers to binary (0 or 1).

exercises.prompt: In summary, what is the main job of the Softmax function in the attention mechanism?

options.text: It multiplies all scores by a factor of 10.
It removes all negative numbers and leaves the positive ones unchanged.
It turns raw scores into probabilities that add up to exactly 1.0.
It randomly picks the single highest score and ignores the rest.

exercises.explain: Softmax is the secret sauce that turns raw comparison scores into clean percentages. It guarantees that our attention weights are easy to work with and always sum to 100%. Next up: Self-Attention.
